data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260515T091251-13788c66/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260515T091251-13788c66/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260515T091251-13788c66/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260515T091251-13788c66/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260515T091251-13788c66/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-7eb97e4e">
    <title>try: surface side-worktree landings with rescue commands</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
During the 2026-05-15 Fizeau grendel-off routing incident, the operator had to run a targeted `ddx try fizeau-a54e0299` from a clean side worktree because the active fizeau checkout was dirty. The worker succeeded and printed `✓ fizeau-a54e0299 → merged (0d70176c)`, but that success was branch-local to the side worktree branch `ddx/a54e0299-burndown-232516`. The active fizeau `master` checkout later advanced independently and still did not contain the route-time health fix. The operator had to inspect git history manually, notice that `master` lacked the landed branch, create a rescue branch, and run `git merge --no-ff ddx/a54e0299-burndown-232516` to make the successful bead reachable from current project history.

This is not a model/routing bug. It is DDx operator UX and audit-safety around side-worktree landings. A successful `ddx try` must make the landing coordinates and recovery command unambiguous when the code lands in a worktree branch that is not the operator's active target branch.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/try.go:503-514` writes the terminal try result but only prints `bead`, `status`, `detail`, `result_rev`, and `preserve_ref`. It omits existing `ImplementationRev` / `LandedRev` fields and has no branch/worktree/rescue summary, so `merged` looks complete even when it landed on a side branch.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1056-1068` resolves the target branch internally, and `LandRequest.TargetBranch` documents the branch contract at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:88-90`, but `LandResult` at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:111-130` does not carry the resolved target branch back to reports.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:397-430` already has `ImplementationRev` and `LandedRev`, and `cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:31-47` already mirrors them. The data exists but the CLI result surface does not expose enough to distinguish worker commit, landed branch tip, and branch-local landing.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:212-220` records unresolvable land conflicts with `preserve_ref`, but it does not attach an operator-ready rescue command such as creating a clean worktree and merging the preserve ref with `--no-ff`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add resolved landing metadata to the success path: `LandResult.TargetBranch`, and propagate it through `ExecuteBeadResult`, `ExecuteBeadReport`, `agent/try.Report`, and the try adapter.
- Update `ddx try` output to always print `implementation_rev` and `landed_rev` when they differ, and to print `landed_branch` / `project_root` for landed attempts.
- When `ddx try` is run from a side worktree or non-default branch, print a clear follow-up block naming the branch-local landing and the no-rewrite rescue command: `git merge --no-ff <landed-branch>` from the desired target worktree, or a `git worktree add ... && git merge --no-ff ...` variant when the active checkout is dirty.
- For `land_conflict_unresolvable` / `land_conflict_operator_required`, include a rescue command in the bead event body and terminal detail using the preserve ref, not just the ref name.
- Keep merge-policy intact: do not squash, rebase, or rewrite execute-bead histories. The rescue path must preserve the worker commits and tracker/evidence commits.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau routing, provider health, or grendel-specific behavior.
- Do not auto-merge side branches into another checked-out dirty worktree.
- Do not change `ddx work` queue ordering or bead priority policy.
- Do not rewrite existing execute-bead branch history.

INTERSECTIONS
- `ddx-06cbaa90` will eventually add a per-project worktree registry. This bead is narrower: expose/rescue the landing coordinates for today's in-tree mode.
- `ddx-c191e91a` covers stale worktree cleanup. This bead is not cleanup; it is successful-result discoverability and manual rescue guidance.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestTryResult_PrintsLandingCoordinates|TestTrySideWorktreeLanding_PrintsNoRewriteRescueCommand' -count=1` passes. The tests prove a successful side-worktree `ddx try` output includes `implementation_rev`, `landed_rev`, `landed_branch`, `project_root`, and a `git merge --no-ff <branch>` rescue command.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestLandResult_CarriesResolvedTargetBranch|TestApplyLandResult_PropagatesTargetBranchToReport' -count=1` passes and proves the resolved branch from `Land()` reaches `ExecuteBeadReport` / `agent/try.Report`.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run TestLandConflictUnresolvableEventIncludesRescueCommand -count=1` passes and proves the parked-conflict event body includes `preserve_ref` plus a no-rewrite rescue command using `git merge --no-ff`.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
Additional rescue observation: after creating rescue branch rescue/a54e0299-route-health-235101, the committed file /tmp/fizeau-a54e0299-rescue.235101/.ddx/beads.jsonl contains fizeau-a54e0299 with status=closed and closing_commit_sha=0d70176c, but running ddx bead show fizeau-a54e0299 from that rescue worktree reported status=open because the installed ddx command resolved state from the active/canonical fizeau checkout instead of the command worktree's committed .ddx file. The fix should print the state root/worktree used by try/bead commands, or otherwise make side-worktree state resolution explicit; this reinforces the need for branch/worktree landing coordinates in the terminal result.
    </notes>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:execution, area:cli, kind:bug, operator-ux, incident:fizeau-route-health-rescue</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-f088a3fd" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="65f95d92cd3303f66260d2a774b4f0e6567cf6fc" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260515T091251-13788c66"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `026e25586dda20184f81046215cd43695d78dc6d` with subject `fix: surface side-worktree landings with rescue commands [ddx-7eb97e4e]`.

`ddx try` now prints `implementation_rev`, `landed_rev`, `landed_branch`, and `project_root` for landed attempts, and it emits a no-rewrite follow-up block with `git merge --no-ff <branch>` for branch-local landings. The resolved landing branch now propagates from `LandResult` through `ExecuteBeadResult`, `ExecuteBeadReport`, and the legacy `agent/try.Report`, and both modern and legacy land-conflict recovery paths now include `rescue_command` text in the bead event body and terminal detail.

Verified with `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestTryResult_PrintsLandingCoordinates|TestTrySideWorktreeLanding_PrintsNoRewriteRescueCommand' -count=1`, `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestLandResult_CarriesResolvedTargetBranch|TestApplyLandResult_PropagatesTargetBranchToReport' -count=1`, `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run TestLandConflictUnresolvableEventIncludesRescueCommand -count=1`, `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1`, and `lefthook run pre-commit`.
